name: Matthew Chase Levy
background: Entrepreneur and internationally recognized scientist. Founder of NobleAI (Science-Based AI) and AE Blue Capital (clean energy venture firm). B.A. from UCLA, Master's and Ph.D. in physics from Rice University. First American physicist awarded the Isaac Newton International Fellowship by the Royal Society at Oxford. Fellow at Wolfson College, Oxford. Trustee of The Washington Institute. Serves on Finance Committee for Mayor of Oakland.
statement: Matthew Chase Levy is a California-based entrepreneur, internationally recognized scientist and running for Governor as a political outsider. He is the founder of NobleAI, a Science-Based AI platform that accelerates discovery and innovation in chemistry, materials, and energy sectors, and AE Blue Capital, a venture firm that backs fusion and other advanced clean-energy technologies critical to addressing California's rising energy costs, grid reliability issues, and long-term decarbonization goals. His experience with these endeavors will drive practical solutions of societal problems, expand employment and maintain California's preeminent role as a world leader in harnessing science for the benefit of all Californians. Levy holds a B.A. from UCLA and Master's and Ph.D. degrees in physics from Rice University. While completing his doctorate, he conducted research at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory on U.S. strategic security. This led to him becoming the first American physicist awarded the Isaac Newton International Fellowship by the Royal Society at Oxford University. Early international experience in Tokyo—before and during his UCLA years—combined with his work in the UK, gives him a unique perspective on global innovation and the challenges facing California families.

positions.economy: Drive practical solutions to societal problems; expand employment; maintain California as global innovation leader
positions.environment: Advanced clean energy technologies; fusion energy; grid reliability; decarbonization
positions.energy: Address rising energy costs via clean tech; AI-driven innovation in chemistry and materials
